column4:
for adults: 
NTT --> social with no interval (6' NTT, remove lateral opaque barriers and record another 6' with fish visual access to a shoal)
NTT --> mirror aggressive response no interval (6' NTT, remove one lateral opaque barrier and record another 6' with fish visual access to a mirror)
social --> mirror response with 1h interval
Open filed --> avoidance with no interval (10'open field followed by negative stimulus and recording for another 10')
for larvae:
open field --> OMR with no interval
OMR --> avoidance with 1h interval
column7: I perform NTT (or open field) first because fish habituate to the tank and I can extract any second response (social, aggression) without the initial anxiety of the tank novelty
column8: when I use an interval, it is 1h
column9: Acclimation to the experimental room is 24h, acclimation to the tank (for example open filed) is 15 min
column11: 20 x 12 x 16 cm

column4: light/dark preference - novel tank diving (tests follow directly on one another)
column7: We focus on anxiety-like behaviour. Should the first test amplify such behaviour (this is not the reason for performing more than one test; any behavioural assessment could potentially cause this), it can be measured in the latter. For this reason, the same order is used in all fish.
column8: In our current induced severe stress model, we test on days 1, 4, 7, 14 and 21 to measure persistence of anxiety-like behaviour.
column9: After acquiring young adult fish: 6 weeks quarantine. Within the lab (dedicated aquatic facility), temperature, water paramaters and light conditions are constant and fish are "acclimated" for ten minutes after moving their tanks from the system to mitigate possible effects of handling the tank. Water from the same system is used in the testing aparatus.
column10: 6 minutes (both light/dark and novel tank diving)
column11: NTT: due to repeated testing (D1, D4, D7, D14, D21) in the same fish, differently shaped tanks are used to maintain novelty of the tank. These tanks have similar volumes.

column2: CSIR-Institute of Genomics and Integrative Biology
column7: The assays which just test for innate behaviour (wherein acclimatization is not required) are performed first then the tests which are memory/ behaviour based (which requires acclimatization / training)

column4: Combined Openfield Light Dark Test followed by a Novel Tank Diving Test . No interval between the tests
column7: I sequence the Combined Open Field and Light-Dark Test before the Novel Tank Test to minimize novelty-induced stress confounds. The combined test allows initial assessment of anxiety-like behavior in a familiar horizontal plane, while the Novel Tank Test, being more stressful due to its vertical exploration component, is done later to avoid influencing earlier results.
column11: Color Preference Test:  Custom 4-arm plus maze tank: 30 cm (length) × 30 cm (width) × 15 cm (height)  Combined Open Field and Light-Dark Test:  Rectangular tank: 24 cm (length) × 18 cm (width) × 14 cm (height)  Divided into equal light and dark zones with opaque and transparent inserts.  Novel Tank Diving Test:  5 tanks with these parameters: 28 cm (top length) × 22 cm (bottom length) × 15 cm (width) × 15 cm (height)
